LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT BASF CORPORATION. CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 15, 2020. PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT BASF CORPORATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO A FURTHER FACTUAL SHOWING. IN EACH OF THE TWO PREVIOUSLY APPROVED AGREEMENTS (THE FIRST WITH AFINIA 3D AND MICROBOARDS TECHNOLOGY LLC AND THE SECOND WITH MAKERBOT INDUSTRIES, LLC), THE SETTLING DEFENDANT(S) AGREED TO REIMBURSE PLAINTIFF $30,000 TO COVER A PORTION OF ITS ATTORNEY'S FEES, INVESTIGATION FEES, AND COSTS INCURRED IN THIS ACTION, FOR A TOTAL OF $60,000. THE CURRENT, THIRD PROPOSED AGREEMENT PROVIDES THAT DEFENDANT BASF WILL PAY PLAINTIFF THE SAME AMOUNT ($30,000) IN FEES AND COSTS, WHICH WOULD BRING THE TOTAL OF FEES AND COSTS PAID IN SETTLEMENT TO $90,000. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUBMITTED ITS COUNSEL'S TIME SHEETS, AND THE DECLARATIONS DO NOT ADDRESS THE ALLOCATION OF PLAINTIFF'S LODESTAR AS AMONG THE DIFFERENT DEFENDANTS OR DISCLOSE WHETHER THERE IS ANY OVERLAP OR DUPLICATION AS TO THE HOURS AND COSTS FOR WHICH PLAINTIFF SEEKS COMPENSATION, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER THE PROPOSED AWARD OF ATTORNEY'S FEES IS REASONABLE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, AS REQUIRED. PLAINTIFF MAY SUBMIT A FURTHER DECLARATION OF COUNSEL, TOGETHER WITH TIME SHEETS SHOWING ITS TOTAL LODESTAR ON THE ENTIRE CASE AND THE ALLOCATION OF TIME AND COSTS DEVOTED TO THE LITIGATION AGAINST EACH OF THE SEPARATE DEFENDANTS SUFFICIENT TO ALLOW THE COURT TO MAKE THAT DETERMINATION. SUCH A DECLARATION SHALL BE FILED BY SEPTEMBER 8, AND A FURTHER HEARING WILL BE HELD ON SEPTEMBER 15, 2020. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendant Basf Corporation
Off Calendar Aug-24-2020 Continued To Sep-15-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendant Basf Corporation.

Continued to September 15, 2020. Plaintiff Ecological Rights Foundation's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement and for entry of consent judgment as to Defendant BASF Corporation is denied without prejudice to a further factual showing. In each of the two previously approved agreements (the first with Afinia 3D and Microboards Technology LLC and the second with Makerbot Industries, LLC), the settling defendant(s) agreed to reimburse Plaintiff $30,000 to cover a portion of its attorney's fees, investigation fees, and costs incurred in this action, for a total of $60,000. The current, third proposed agreement provides that Defendant BASF will pay Plaintiff the same amount ($30,000) in fees and costs, which would bring the total of fees and costs paid in settlement to $90,000. Because Plaintiff has not submitted its counsel's time sheets, and the declarations do not address the allocation of Plaintiff's lodestar as among the different defendants or disclose whether there is any overlap or duplication as to the hours and costs for which Plaintiff seeks compensation, the Court cannot determine whether the proposed award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law, as required. Plaintiff may submit a further declaration of counsel, together with time sheets showing its total lodestar on the entire case and the allocation of time and costs devoted to the litigation against each of the separate defendants sufficient to allow the Court to make that determination. Such a declaration shall be filed by September 8, and a further hearing will be held on September 15, 2020.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
